notes: Memory-only Lite Demo package with one public skill.

v0.2.17 preserves Lite Demo governance, user boundaries, old-memory takeover, and lightweight local Markdown use while adding Lite-native storage/retrieval separation through owner routes and derived pointer cache.
Old users upgrade the skill program files safely; project memories are not deleted or migrated during install.

On first activation in an old project, Lite Demo asks once: inherit and reorganize old memory, or start fresh. Inheritance only edits memory files and does not resume old tasks or touch business files.

If inheritance is requested but no current project root exposes old memory, Codex first performs bounded candidate discovery and presents choices instead of asking ordinary users to locate docs/codex by hand; broader discovery remains opt-in.
session-log.md remains a sparse in-flight queue. Settled entries discharge byte-preserved, and recovery starts from compact routes and owners rather than default log injection.
Long local text sources are sliced into artifacts while memory owners keep interpretation, routes, and continuation guards.
Standalone context-memory-index copies are archived out of active skills with restore metadata; Lite Demo remains one public skill.
The on-disk memory schema/checkpoint compatibility label remains v0.2.7; package version text alone never proves takeover completion.
v0.1.7 remains available as the retained stable fallback.
